  [user_task_description] # Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application  ## 1. Objective Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.  ## 2. Language The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.  ## 3. Page Design  The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard - **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities. - **Elements**:   - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.   - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.   - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.   - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.   - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.  ### 2. Current Weather Page - **Page Title**: Current Weather - **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location. - **Elements**:   - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.   - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.   - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.   - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).   - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.   - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.  ### 3. Weekly Forecast Page - **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast - **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days. - **Elements**:   - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.   - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.   - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.   - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 4. Location Search Page - **Page Title**: Search Locations - **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations. - **Elements**:   - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.   - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.   - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.   - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).   - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.  ### 5. Weather Alerts Page - **Page Title**: Weather Alerts - **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations. - **Elements**:   - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.   - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.   - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).   - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.   - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).  ### 6. Air Quality Page - **Page Title**: Air Quality Index - **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations. - **Elements**:   - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.   - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).   - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).   - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.   - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.   - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.  ### 7. Saved Locations Page - **Page Title**: Saved Locations - **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access. - **Elements**:   - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.   - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.   - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).   - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).   - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.  ### 8. Settings Page - **Page Title**: Settings - **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location. - **Elements**:   - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.   - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).   - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.   - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.   - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ## 4. Data Storage  The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. Current Weather Data - **File Name**: `current_weather.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30   2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30   3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30   ```  ### 2. Forecasts Data - **File Name**: `forecasts.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60   2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70   3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85   ```  ### 3. Locations Data - **File Name**: `locations.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST   2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT   3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST   ```  ### 4. Weather Alerts Data - **File Name**: `alerts.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0   2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0   3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1   ```  ### 5. Air Quality Data - **File Name**: `air_quality.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30   2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30   3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30   ```  ### 6. Saved Locations Data - **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|1|New York|1   2|1|2|London|0   3|2|3|Tokyo|1   ```

Current Weather Data\n- File path: `data/current_weather.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. location_id (int) - Unique ID of the location\n  2. location_name (str) - Name of the location\n  3. temperature (float) - Temperature value\n  4. condition (str) - Weather condition (e.g., Sunny, Rainy)\n  5. humidity (int) - Humidity percentage\n  6. wind_speed (float) - Wind speed\n  7. last_updated (str) - DateTime string in format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30\n2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30\n3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30\n```\n\n### 2. Forecasts Data\n- File path: `data/forecasts.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. forecast_id (int) - Unique forecast record ID\n  2. location_id (int) - Location ID\n  3. date (str) - Date of forecast (YYYY-MM-DD)\n  4. high_temp (float) - High temperature\n  5. low_temp (float) - Low temperature\n  6. condition (str) - Forecasted weather condition\n  7. precipitation (int) - Precipitation percentage\n  8. humidity (int) - Humidity percentage\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60\n2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70\n3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85\n```\n\n### 3. Locations Data\n- File path: `data/locations.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. location_id (int) - Unique location ID\n  2. location_name (str) - Location name\n  3. latitude (float) - Latitude coordinate\n  4. longitude (float) - Longitude coordinate\n  5. country (str) - Country name\n  6. timezone (str) - Timezone string\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST\n2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT\n3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST\n```\n\n### 4. Weather Alerts Data\n- File path: `data/alerts.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. alert_id (int) - Unique alert ID\n  2. location_id (int) - Location ID\n  3. alert_type (str) - Type of alert (e.g., Thunderstorm, Fog)\n  4. severity (str) - Severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low)\n  5. description (str) - Alert description\n  6. start_time (str) - Start date/time (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)\n  7. end_time (str) - End date/time (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)\n  8. is_acknowledged (int) - 0 if not acknowledged, 1 if acknowledged\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0\n2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0\n3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1\n```\n\n### 5. Air Quality Data\n- File path: `data/air_quality.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. aqi_id (int) - Unique air quality record ID\n  2. location_id (int) - Location ID\n  3. aqi_index (int) - Air Quality Index (0-500)\n  4. pm25 (float) - PM2.5 pollution level\n  5. pm10 (float) - PM10 pollution level\n  6. no2 (float) - NO2 pollution level\n  7. o3 (float) - O3 pollution level\n  8. last_updated (str) - DateTime string (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30\n2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30\n3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30\n```\n\n### 6. Saved Locations Data\n- File path: `data/saved_locations.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. saved_id (int) - Unique saved location entry ID\n  2. user_id (int) - User identifier (always 1 since no auth)\n  3. location_id (int) - Location ID\n  4. location_name (str) - Location name\n  5. is_default (int) - 1 if default location, 0 otherwise\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|1|1|New York|1\n2|1|2|London|0\n3|2|3|Tokyo|1\n```\n\n---\n\nEnd of Design Specification\n"})

# Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.
  - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.
  - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.
  - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.

### 2. Current Weather Page
- **Page Title**: Current Weather
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.
  - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.
  - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.
  - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).
  - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.
  - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.

### 3. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast
- **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.
  - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.
  - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.
  - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Location Search Page
- **Page Title**: Search Locations
- **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.
  - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.
  - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.
  - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).
  - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.

### 5. Weather Alerts Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Alerts
- **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.
  - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.
  - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).
  - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.
  - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).

### 6. Air Quality Page
- **Page Title**: Air Quality Index
- **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.
  - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).
  - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).
  - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.
  - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.
  - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.

### 7. Saved Locations Page
- **Page Title**: Saved Locations
- **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.
  - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.
  - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).
  - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).
  - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.

### 8. Settings Page
- **Page Title**: Settings
- **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.
  - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).
  - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.
  - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Current Weather Data
- **File Name**: `current_weather.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 2. Forecasts Data
- **File Name**: `forecasts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
  ```

### 3. Locations Data
- **File Name**: `locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
  ```

### 4. Weather Alerts Data
- **File Name**: `alerts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
  ```

### 5. Air Quality Data
- **File Name**: `air_quality.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 6. Saved Locations Data
- **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1|New York|1
  2|1|2|London|0
  3|2|3|Tokyo|1
  ```

# Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.
  - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.
  - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.
  - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.

### 2. Current Weather Page
- **Page Title**: Current Weather
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.
  - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.
  - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.
  - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).
  - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.
  - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.

### 3. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast
- **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.
  - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.
  - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.
  - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Location Search Page
- **Page Title**: Search Locations
- **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.
  - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.
  - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.
  - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).
  - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.

### 5. Weather Alerts Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Alerts
- **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.
  - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.
  - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).
  - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.
  - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).

### 6. Air Quality Page
- **Page Title**: Air Quality Index
- **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.
  - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).
  - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).
  - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.
  - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.
  - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.

### 7. Saved Locations Page
- **Page Title**: Saved Locations
- **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.
  - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.
  - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).
  - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).
  - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.

### 8. Settings Page
- **Page Title**: Settings
- **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.
  - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).
  - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.
  - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Current Weather Data
- **File Name**: `current_weather.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 2. Forecasts Data
- **File Name**: `forecasts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
  ```

### 3. Locations Data
- **File Name**: `locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
  ```

### 4. Weather Alerts Data
- **File Name**: `alerts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
  ```

### 5. Air Quality Data
- **File Name**: `air_quality.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 6. Saved Locations Data
- **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1|New York|1
  2|1|2|London|0
  3|2|3|Tokyo|1
  ```

Template: `templates/current_weather.html`\n- Page Title: Current Weather\n- Main Heading: `<h1 id=\"location-name\">{{ location_name }}</h1>`\n- Element IDs:\n  - `current-weather-page` (Div): container for the page\n  - `location-name` (H1): displays location name\n  - `temperature-display` (Div): displays temperature\n  - `weather-condition` (Div): displays weather condition\n  - `humidity-info` (Div): displays humidity percentage\n  - `wind-speed-info` (Div): displays wind speed\n- Navigation:\n  - No explicit navigation defined; frontend may implement a back button\n- Context variables:\n  - `location_name`: str\n  - `temperature`: float\n  - `condition`: str\n  - `humidity`: int\n  - `wind_speed`: float\n- Usage notes:\n  - Render data inside respective elements using the IDs\n\n### 3. Template: `templates/weekly_forecast.html`\n- Page Title: Weekly Forecast\n- Main Heading: `<h1>Weekly Forecast</h1>`\n- Element IDs:\n  - `forecast-page` (Div): container for the page\n  - `forecast-table` (Table): displays daily forecast rows with columns: date, high temp, low temp, condition\n  - `location-filter` (Dropdown): filter forecasts by location\n  - `forecast-list` (Div): grid displaying forecast cards for each day\n  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button): navigates back to Dashboard\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `location-filter`: onchange should trigger reload or rerender with selected location\n  - `back-to-dashboard` -> `url_for('dashboard')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `forecasts`: list of dicts as specified\n  - `locations`: list of dicts with `location_id` and `location_name`\n  - `selected_location_id`: int or None\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop through `forecasts` to build forecast table rows or cards\n  - Populate `location-filter` dropdown with `locations`\n\n### 4. Template: `templates/location_search.html`\n- Page Title: Search Locations\n- Main Heading: `<h1>Search Locations</h1>`\n- Element IDs:\n  - `search-page` (Div): container for the page\n  - `location-search-input` (Input): text input for search by city name or coordinates\n  - `search-results` (Div): container listing matching search results\n  - `select-location-button-{location_id}` (Button): one per result row with dynamic ID pattern\n  - `saved-locations-list` (Div): displaying saved locations\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Each `select-location-button-{location_id}` triggers a POST to select location\n- Context variables:\n  - `search_query`: str\n  - `search_results`: list of dicts with location info\n  - `saved_locations`: list of dicts with location info\n- Usage notes:\n  - Use loops to render search results and saved locations\n  - Use dynamic element IDs: `select-location-button-{location_id}`\n\n### 5. Template: `templates/alerts.html`\n- Page Title: Weather Alerts\n- Main Heading: `<h1>Weather Alerts</h1>`\n- Element IDs:\n  - `alerts-page` (Div): container for the page\n  - `alerts-list` (Div): list of all active alerts\n  - `severity-filter` (Dropdown): filter alerts by severity\n  - `location-filter-alerts` (Dropdown): filter alerts by location\n  - `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}` (Button): acknowledge alert, dynamic ID per alert\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Each `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}` sends POST to `/alerts/acknowledge/<alert_id>`\n- Context variables:\n  - `alerts`: list of alert dicts\n  - `severity_filter`: str\n  - `location_filter`: int or None\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop alerts to render each alert\n  - Use dynamic element IDs: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}`\n\n### 6. Template: `templates/air_quality.html`\n- Page Title: Air Quality Index\n- Main Heading: `<h1>Air Quality Index</h1>`\n- Element IDs:\n  - `air-quality-page` (Div): page container\n  - `aqi-display` (Div): displays AQI index (0-500)\n  - `aqi-description` (Div): displays AQI description text\n  - `pollution-details` (Table): shows PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3 levels\n  - `location-aqi-filter` (Dropdown): filter air quality by location\n  - `health-recommendation` (Div): health tips based on AQI\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `location-aqi-filter`: triggers refresh with selected location\n- Context variables:\n  - `air_quality_data`: list of dicts\n  - `locations`: list of location dicts\n  - `selected_location_id`: int or None\n  - `health_recommendation`: str\n- Usage notes:\n  - Render pollutant levels in table rows\n  - Use dropdown to filter air quality data\n\n### 7. Template: `templates/saved_locations.html`\n- Page Title: Saved Locations\n- Main Heading: `<h1>Saved Locations</h1>`\n- Element IDs:\n  - `saved-locations-page` (Div): container\n  - `locations-table` (Table): displays saved locations, temp and weather\n  - `view-location-weather-{location_id}` (Button): view weather for location, dynamic ID\n  - `remove-location-button-{location_id}` (Button): remove saved location, dynamic ID\n  - `add-new-location-button` (Button): add new location\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `view-location-weather-{location_id}` -> `url_for('current_weather', location_id=location_id)`\n  - `remove-location-button-{location_id}` triggers delete (POST or AJAX)\n  - `add-new-location-button` -> `url_for('location_search')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `saved_locations`: list of saved locations dict\n  - `current_weather_map`: dict mapping location_id to dict with temperature and condition\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop through saved locations to build table rows\n  - Use dynamic IDs for buttons\n\n### 8. Template: `templates/settings.html`\n- Page Title: Settings\n- Main Heading: `<h1>Settings</h1>`\n- Element IDs:\n  - `settings-page` (Div): container\n  - `temperature-unit-select` (Dropdown): select temperature unit\n  - `default-location-select` (Dropdown): select default location\n  - `alert-notifications-toggle` (Checkbox): enable/disable alert notifications\n  - `save-settings-button` (Button): save settings\n  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button): navigate back to dashboard\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `save-settings-button` submits POST to `/settings`\n  - `back-to-dashboard` -> `url_for('dashboard')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `temperature_unit`: str\n  - `default_location_id`: int\n  - `alert_notifications_enabled`: bool\n  - `locations`: list of dicts with `location_id` and `location_name`\n- Usage notes:\n  - Populate dropdowns with locations and units\n  - Bind checkbox for notifications\n\n---\n\n## Section 3: Data File Schemas\n\n### 1. Current Weather Data\n- File: `data/current_weather.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited, order is strict):\n  1. `location_id` (int) - Unique identifier for location\n  2. `location_name` (str) - Name of the location\n  3. `temperature` (float) - Current temperature\n  4. `condition` (str) - Weather condition (e.g., Sunny, Rainy)\n  5. `humidity` (int) - Humidity percentage\n  6. `wind_speed` (float) - Wind speed\n  7. `last_updated` (str) - Timestamp string in \"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM\" format\n- Example lines:\n  ```\n  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30\n  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30\n  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30\n  ```\n\n### 2. Forecasts Data\n- File: `data/forecasts.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. `forecast_id` (int) - Unique forecast entry ID\n  2. `location_id` (int) - Associated location ID\n  3. `date` (str) - Date in \"YYYY-MM-DD\"\n  4. `high_temp` (float) - High temperature forecast\n  5. `low_temp` (float) - Low temperature forecast\n  6. `condition` (str) - Forecast condition\n  7. `precipitation` (int) - Precipitation percent\n  8. `humidity` (int) - Humidity percentage\n- Example lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60\n  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70\n  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85\n  ```\n\n### 3. Locations Data\n- File: `data/locations.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. `location_id` (int) - Unique location ID\n  2. `location_name` (str) - Location name\n  3. `latitude` (float) - Latitude coordinate\n  4. `longitude` (float) - Longitude coordinate\n  5. `country` (str) - Country name\n  6. `timezone` (str) - Timezone string\n- Example lines:\n  ```\n  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST\n  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT\n  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST\n  ```\n\n### 4. Weather Alerts Data\n- File: `data/alerts.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. `alert_id` (int) - Unique alert ID\n  2. `location_id` (int) - Location ID\n  3. `alert_type` (str) - Type of alert\n  4. `severity` (str) - Severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low)\n  5. `description` (str) - Alert description\n  6. `start_time` (str) - Start date/time (\"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM\")\n  7. `end_time` (str) - End date/time (\"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM\")\n  8. `is_acknowledged` (int) - 0 = not acknowledged, 1 = acknowledged\n- Example lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0\n  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0\n  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1\n  ```\n\n### 5. Air Quality Data\n- File: `data/air_quality.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. `aqi_id` (int) - Unique air quality record ID\n  2. `location_id` (int) - Location ID\n  3. `aqi_index` (int) - Air Quality Index value (0-500)\n  4. `pm25` (float) - PM2.5 pollution level\n  5. `pm10` (float) - PM10 pollution level\n  6. `no2` (float) - NO2 pollution level\n  7. `o3` (float) - O3 pollution level\n  8. `last_updated` (str) - DateTime string \"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM\"\n- Example lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30\n  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30\n  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30\n  ```\n\n### 6. Saved Locations Data\n- File: `data/saved_locations.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. `saved_id` (int) - Unique saved location entry ID\n  2. `user_id` (int) - User identifier (always 1 since no auth)\n  3. `location_id` (int) - Location ID\n  4. `location_name` (str) - Location name\n  5. `is_default` (int) - 1 if default location, 0 otherwise\n- Example lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|1|New York|1\n  2|1|2|London|0\n  3|2|3|Tokyo|1\n  ```\n\n---\n\nEnd of Design Specification\n"})

# Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.
  - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.
  - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.
  - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.

### 2. Current Weather Page
- **Page Title**: Current Weather
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.
  - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.
  - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.
  - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).
  - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.
  - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.

### 3. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast
- **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.
  - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.
  - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.
  - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Location Search Page
- **Page Title**: Search Locations
- **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.
  - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.
  - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.
  - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).
  - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.

### 5. Weather Alerts Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Alerts
- **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.
  - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.
  - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).
  - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.
  - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).

### 6. Air Quality Page
- **Page Title**: Air Quality Index
- **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.
  - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).
  - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).
  - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.
  - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.
  - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.

### 7. Saved Locations Page
- **Page Title**: Saved Locations
- **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.
  - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.
  - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).
  - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).
  - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.

### 8. Settings Page
- **Page Title**: Settings
- **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.
  - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).
  - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.
  - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Current Weather Data
- **File Name**: `current_weather.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 2. Forecasts Data
- **File Name**: `forecasts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
  ```

### 3. Locations Data
- **File Name**: `locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
  ```

### 4. Weather Alerts Data
- **File Name**: `alerts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
  ```

### 5. Air Quality Data
- **File Name**: `air_quality.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 6. Saved Locations Data
- **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1|New York|1
  2|1|2|London|0
  3|2|3|Tokyo|1
  ```

# Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.
  - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.
  - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.
  - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.

### 2. Current Weather Page
- **Page Title**: Current Weather
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location.
- **Elements**:

  - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.
  - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.
  - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).

  - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.

### 6. Air Quality Page

  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Current Weather Data
- **File Name**: `current_weather.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 2. Forecasts Data
- **File Name**: `forecasts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
  ```

### 3. Locations Data
- **File Name**: `locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
  ```

### 4. Weather Alerts Data
- **File Name**: `alerts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
  ```

### 5. Air Quality Data
- **File Name**: `air_quality.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 6. Saved Locations Data
- **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1|New York|1
  2|1|2|London|0
  3|2|3|Tokyo|1
  ```

=== user_task_description ===

# Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.
  - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.
  - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.
  - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.

### 2. Current Weather Page
- **Page Title**: Current Weather
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.
  - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.
  - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.
  - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).
  - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.
  - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.

### 3. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast
- **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.
  - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.
  - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.
  - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Location Search Page
- **Page Title**: Search Locations
- **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.
  - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.
  - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.
  - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).
  - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.

### 5. Weather Alerts Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Alerts
- **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.
  - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.
  - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).
  - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.
  - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).

### 6. Air Quality Page
- **Page Title**: Air Quality Index
- **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.
  - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).
  - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).
  - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.
  - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.
  - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.

### 7. Saved Locations Page
- **Page Title**: Saved Locations
- **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.
  - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.
  - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).
  - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).
  - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.

### 8. Settings Page
- **Page Title**: Settings
- **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.
  - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).
  - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.
  - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Current Weather Data
- **File Name**: `current_weather.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 2. Forecasts Data
- **File Name**: `forecasts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
  ```

### 3. Locations Data
- **File Name**: `locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
  ```

### 4. Weather Alerts Data
- **File Name**: `alerts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
  ```

### 5. Air Quality Data
- **File Name**: `air_quality.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 6. Saved Locations Data
- **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1|New York|1
  2|1|2|London|0
  3|2|3|Tokyo|1
  ```

str (only for POST, optional)\nsearch_results: list of dict {\n  location_id: int,\n  location_name: str,\n  latitude: float or str,\n  longitude: float or str,\n  country: str,\n  timezone:                 |\n| /alerts                      | GET          | weather_alerts            | alerts.html                   | alerts: list of dict {\n  alert_id: int,\n  location_id: int,\n  alert_type: str,\n  severity: str (values: Critical, High, Medium, Low),\n  description: str,\n  start_time: str (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM),\n  end_time: str (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM),\n  is_acknowledged: bool\n}\nseverity_filter: str (values: All, Critical, High, Medium, Low)\nlocation_filter: int or None                                                                                                  |\n| /alerts/acknowledge/<int:alert_id> | POST         | acknowledge_alert         | None (redirect back or JSON response) | alert_id: int (from URL)\n\nNote: This route handles POST request to mark alert as acknowledged and does not render a template.|  \n| /airquality                  | GET          | air_quality_page          | air_quality.html              | air_quality_data: list of dict {\n  aqi_id: int,\n  location_id: int,\n  aqi_index: int,\n  pm25: float,\n  pm10: float,\n  no2: float,\n  o3: float,\n  last_updated: str (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)\n}\nlocations: list of dict {\n  location_id: int,\n  location_name: str\n}\nselected_location_id: int or None\nhealth_recommendation: str                                                                                                 |\n| /locations/saved             | GET          | saved_locations           | saved_locations.html          | saved_locations: list of dict {\n  saved_id: int,\n  user_id: int (always 1 as no auth),\n  location_id: int,\n  location_name: str,\n  is_default: bool\n}\ncurrent_weather_map: dict { location_id (int): dict {\n  temperature: float,\n  condition: str\n}}\n                                                                                                                                                      |\n| /settings                   | GET, POST   | settings                 | settings.html                 | temperature_unit: str (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin)\ndefault_location_id: int\nalert_notifications_enabled: bool\nlocations: list of dict {\n  location_id: int,\n  location_name: str\n}\n\n---\n\n## Section 2: Frontend HTML Templates Specification\n\n### 1. templates/dashboard.html\n- Page Title: Weather Dashboard\n- Main heading: <h1>Weather Dashboard</h1> (implicit in dashboard page container if used)\n- Element IDs and types:\n  - dashboard-page: Div container for the dashboard page\n  - current-weather-summary: Div containing summary of the current weather for default location\n  - search-location-button: Button to navigate to location search page\n  - view-forecast-button: Button to navigate to weekly forecast page\n  - view-alerts-button: Button to navigate to weather alerts page\n- Navigation mappings via url_for:\n  - search-location-button -> url_for('location_search')\n  - view-forecast-button -> url_for('weekly_forecast')\n  - view-alerts-button -> url_for('weather_alerts')\n- Context variables:\n  - current_weather: dict with fields:\n      location_id: int\n      location_name: str\n      temperature: float\n      condition: str\n      humidity: int\n      wind_speed: float\n      last_updated: str\n- Usage notes:\n  - Render current_weather details inside current-weather-summary div\n\n### 2. templates/current_weather.html\n- Page Title: Current Weather\n- Main heading: <h1 id=\"location-name\">{{ location_name }}</h1>\n- Element IDs and types:\n  - current-weather-page: Div container\n  - location-name: H1 showing location name\n  - temperature-display: Div showing temperature\n  - weather-condition: Div showing weather condition\n  - humidity-info: Div showing humidity percentage\n  - wind-speed-info: Div showing wind speed\n- Navigation:\n  - Possibly a back button added by frontend; no route explicitly specified\n- Context variables:\n  - location_name: str\n  - temperature: float\n  - condition: str\n  - humidity: int\n  - wind_speed: float\n- Usage notes:\n  - Render these fields in respective divs identified by the given IDs\n\n### 3. templates/weekly_forecast.html\n- Page Title: Weekly Forecast\n- Main heading: <h1>Weekly Forecast</h1>\n- Element IDs and types:\n  - forecast-page: Div container\n  - forecast-table: Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, condition\n  - location-filter: Dropdown to filter forecast by location\n  - forecast-list: Div as grid for forecast cards\n  - back-to-dashboard: Button to navigate back to dashboard\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - location-filter selects location, sends request to /forecast/weekly with selected_location_id parameter\n  - back-to-dashboard -> url_for('dashboard')\n- Context variables:\n  - forecasts: list of dict (see specs)\n  - locations: list of dict with location_id and location_name\n  - selected_location_id: int or None\n- Usage notes:\n  - Use loops to render forecast rows/cards\n  - Dropdown bound to location options\n\n### 4. templates/location_search.html\n- Page Title: Search Locations\n- Main heading: <h1>Search Locations</h1>\n- Element IDs and types:\n  - search-page: Div container\n  - location-search-input: Input field for user to type search queries\n  - search-results: Div listing matching locations\n  - select-location-button-{location_id}: Buttons for each result with dynamic ID for selecting location\n  - saved-locations-list: Div listing previously saved locations\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - select-location-button-{location_id}: Triggers a POST form or AJAX to select location\n  - search-location-button triggers navigation handled on dashboard\n- Context variables:\n  - search_query: str (possibly empty)\n  - search_results: list of dict with detailed location info\n  - saved_locations: list of dict with location_id and location_name\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop through search_results to display each location with select button\n  - Loop through saved_locations to display saved locations\n  - Use dynamic ID pattern for select-location-button-{location_id}\n\n### 5. templates/alerts.html\n- Page Title: Weather Alerts\n- Main heading: <h1>Weather Alerts</h1>\n- Element IDs and types:\n  - alerts-page: Div container\n  - alerts-list: Div listing all active weather alerts\n  - severity-filter: Dropdown to filter by severity\n  - location-filter-alerts: Dropdown to filter by location\n  - acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}: Buttons for acknowledging each alert with dynamic ID\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}: sends POST to /alerts/acknowledge/<alert_id>\n- Context variables:\n  - alerts: list of dict with alert details\n  - severity_filter: current filter string\n  - location_filter: current location filter id or None\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop through alerts to display details\n  - Use dynamic ID pattern for acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}\n\n### 6. templates/air_quality.html\n- Page Title: Air Quality Index\n- Main heading: <h1>Air Quality Index</h1>\n- Element IDs and types:\n  - air-quality-page: Div container\n  - aqi-display: Div showing AQI index value\n  - aqi-description: Div showing AQI description\n  - pollution-details: Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3 levels\n  - location-aqi-filter: Dropdown for location filtering\n  - health-recommendation: Div showing health recommendations\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - location-aqi-filter selects location and reloads page\n- Context variables:\n  - air_quality_data: list of dict with air quality details\n  - locations: list of dict with location_id and location_name\n  - selected_location_id: int or None\n  - health_recommendation: str\n- Usage notes:\n  - Render pollution details in table rows\n  - Dropdown with locations for filtering\n\n### 7. templates/saved_locations.html\n- Page Title: Saved Locations\n- Main heading: <h1>Saved Locations</h1>\n- Element IDs and types:\n  - saved-locations-page: Div container\n  - locations-table: Table showing saved locations with current temperature and condition\n  - view-location-weather-{location_id}: Buttons to view weather for each location (dynamic ID)\n  - remove-location-button-{location_id}: Buttons to remove location (dynamic ID)\n  - add-new-location-button: Button to add new location\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - view-location-weather-{location_id} -> url_for('current_weather', location_id=location_id)\n  - remove-location-button-{location_id} triggers delete action (handled by POST or AJAX)\n  - add-new-location-button -> url_for('location_search')\n- Context variables:\n  - saved_locations: list of dict\n  - current_weather_map: dict mapping location_id to dict with temperature and condition\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop through saved_locations to display table rows\n  - Use dynamic IDs pattern\n\n### 8. templates/settings.html\n- Page Title: Settings\n- Main heading: <h1>Settings</h1>\n- Element IDs and types:\n  - settings-page: Div container\n  - temperature-unit-select: Dropdown for temperature units (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin)\n  - default-location-select: Dropdown to select default location\n  - alert-notifications-toggle: Checkbox for enabling/disabling alert notifications\n  - save-settings-button: Button to save settings\n  - back-to-dashboard: Button to return to dashboard\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - save-settings-button submits POST form to /settings\n  - back-to-dashboard -> url_for('dashboard')\n- Context variables:\n  - temperature_unit: str\n  - default_location_id: int\n  - alert_notifications_enabled: bool\n  - locations: list of dict (location_id, location_name)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Populate dropdowns with locations and units\n  - Bind checkbox input\n\n---\n\n## Section 3: Data File Schemas\n\n### 1. Current Weather Data\n- File path: `data/current_weather.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. location_id (int) - Unique ID of the location\n  2. location_name (str) - Name of the location\n  3. temperature (float) - Temperature value\n  4. condition (str) - Weather condition (e.g., Sunny, Rainy)\n  5. humidity (int) - Humidity percentage\n  6. wind_speed (float) - Wind speed\n  7. last_updated (str) - DateTime string in format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30\n2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30\n3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30\n```\n\n### 2. Forecasts Data\n- File path: `data/forecasts.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. forecast_id (int) - Unique forecast record ID\n  2. location_id (int) - Location ID\n  3. date (str) - Date of forecast (YYYY-MM-DD)\n  4. high_temp (float) - High temperature\n  5. low_temp (float) - Low temperature\n  6. condition (str) - Forecasted weather condition\n  7. precipitation (int) - Precipitation percentage\n  8. humidity (int) - Humidity percentage\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60\n2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70\n3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85\n```\n\n### 3. Locations Data\n- File path: `data/locations.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. location_id (int) - Unique location ID\n  2. location_name (str) - Location name\n  3. latitude (float) - Latitude coordinate\n  4. longitude (float) - Longitude coordinate\n  5. country (str) - Country name\n  6. timezone (str) - Timezone string\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST\n2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT\n3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST\n```\n\n### 4. Weather Alerts Data\n- File path: `data/alerts.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. alert_id (int) - Unique alert ID\n  2. location_id (int) - Location ID\n  3. alert_type (str) - Type of alert (e.g., Thunderstorm, Fog)\n  4. severity (str) - Severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low)\n  5. description (str) - Alert description\n  6. start_time (str) - Start date/time (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)\n  7. end_time (str) - End date/time (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)\n  8. is_acknowledged (int) - 0 if not acknowledged, 1 if acknowledged\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0\n2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0\n3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1\n```\n\n### 5. Air Quality Data\n- File path: `data/air_quality.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. aqi_id (int) - Unique air quality record ID\n  2. location_id (int) - Location ID\n  3. aqi_index (int) - Air Quality Index (0-500)\n  4. pm25 (float) - PM2.5 pollution level\n  5. pm10 (float) - PM10 pollution level\n  6. no2 (float) - NO2 pollution level\n  7. o3 (float) - O3 pollution level\n  8. last_updated (str) - DateTime string (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30\n2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30\n3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30\n```\n\n### 6. Saved Locations Data\n- File path: `data/saved_locations.txt`\n- Fields (pipe `|` delimited):\n  1. saved_id (int) - Unique saved location entry ID\n  2. user_id (int) - User identifier (always 1 since no auth)\n  3. location_id (int) - Location ID\n  4. location_name (str) - Location name\n  5. is_default (int) - 1 if default location, 0 otherwise\n- Example lines:\n```\n1|1|1|New York|1\n2|1|2|London|0\n3|2|3|Tokyo|1\n```\n\n---\n\nEnd of Design Specification\n"})
